summary: Soundboard recording with variable sound quality but a memorable Playing in the Band and an exquisite Comes A Time solo.
review: The show is praised for its loose, entertaining vibe, highlighted by a soaring Playing in the Band that opened the set and Jerry Garcia’s beautifully precise solo on Comes A Time. The soundboard source is usable, though listeners note uneven balance – the first set sounds clear while the second set can be trebly and occasionally harsh. Overall the performance outweighs the modest recording quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is generally praised (standout songs, strong energy) while recording quality receives mixed feedback (variable clarity, treble issues), yielding a solid but not outstanding combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Variable sound – first set clear, second set has excessive treble that can cause listener fatigue. Overall decent but not polished; file‑list errors noted but unrelated to audio.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set sounds tighter, second set a bit harsher in tone but maintains the show’s loose, entertaining flow; Playing in the Band opens strong and the encore includes playful banter.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His solo on Comes A Time is described as some of his most perfect picking ever.
band_member_comments.Drums: Comment that the drummers have ‘figured it out,’ indicating solid percussion despite variable mix.
